conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I hold 1 unit of X, which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. My goal is to sell this object for a profit, meaning I need a price strictly greater than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first turn, I will propose a price of 50 ZUP. This gives me a 10 ZUP profit while leaving some room for negotiation if Blue wants a better deal. I cannot accept anything at or below 40 ZUP as that would result in a loss or break-even, which doesn't maximize my utility. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of X that cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

Since I have used only 1 of my 3 allowed proposals, I should try to negotiate a price closer to my original target. I will propose a middle ground of 47 ZUP. This is still a profit for me and closer to my goal than 45, but lower than my initial 50 to show flexibility. If they reject this, I will have to decide if 45 is acceptable on the next turn (since I would have used 2 proposals, leaving only 1 more or forcing an accept/reject). Actually, if I propose 47, that is proposal #2. If they reject, I have 1 proposal left. If they counter, I can accept or reject. Let's aim for 47. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 47 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate your offer of 45 ZUP. However, since my initial price was 50 ZUP, I can meet you halfway at 47 ZUP. This still gives me a profit and gets us closer to an agreement. Do you accept 47 ZUP? </message>

<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 46 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 46 ZUP yields a 6 ZUP profit. My previous proposal was 47 ZUP. I have used 1 proposal so far (my initial 50 ZUP offer). This is the second turn where I am responding. If I make another proposal now (e.g., countering 46 with 46.5 - wait, must be integer, so 47 again or 46?), I would use my 2nd proposal. If I accept 46, the game ends with a 6 ZUP profit. If I reject, the game ends with 0 profit. If I propose again, I risk them rejecting or countering lower, and I only have 1 proposal left after that. Since 46 is very close to my previous 47 and ensures a profit, and I want to secure the deal before running out of proposal options or risking a stalemate, I should accept this offer. 6 ZUP profit is better than 0. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
